prompt: Run a data quality audit on our Big Island draft listings. I want to know what's blocking us from publishing them — missing fields, bad modalities, duplicates, whatever. Give me the worst problems first with SQL to fix them.

assertions.name: uses-structured-format
assertions.description: Output uses a structured report format with sections (executive summary, issues by severity, etc.) rather than free-form text

assertions.name: identifies-non-canonical-modalities
assertions.description: Specifically identifies non-canonical modality values (like lowercase or synonym variants)

assertions.name: provides-sql-fixes
assertions.description: Includes runnable SQL statements to fix identified issues

assertions.name: reports-field-completeness
assertions.description: Reports field completeness as percentages (email %, phone %, bio %, photo %)

assertions.name: detects-duplicates
assertions.description: Identifies potential duplicate listings (by phone, website, or name similarity)

assertions.name: uses-correct-column-names
assertions.description: Uses 'bio' for practitioners and 'description' for centers (not the other way around)
